steps.title: Bracket the modifier
steps.content: The relative clause begins with "that" and needs "emphasizes" as its main verb: "an approach that emphasizes retaining." The phrase about aiming to teach music is an interrupting participial modifier, so it needs commas around it.

steps.title: Reject verb conflicts
steps.content: A inserts an extra finite verb. C and D make the aiming verb finite, leaving "emphasizes" as a second uncoordinated verb inside the relative clause. The participial form keeps the structure grammatical. The answer is B.
